sections.title: Cold Weather Operations Safety Checklist
sections.html:
MH CONSTRUCTION, INC.
Industrial Safety & Health Program (MISH)
FORM-MISH-42: Cold Weather Operations Safety Checklist
Rev. 3 | Effective: 04/07/2026 | Safety Officer: Matt Ramsey

Inspection Frequency Note: Inspections must be conducted prior to the start of each shift during cold weather operations, as required by WAC 296-155-110 and WAC 296-62-095.

Item #

Inspection Item

Pass

Fail

N/A

Corrective Action Required

1

Workers are trained on cold stress hazards and symptoms (WAC 296-62-095)

☐

☐

☐

2

Appropriate cold weather PPE is provided and worn (WAC 296-155-200)

☐

☐

☐

3

Warm areas are available for breaks (WAC 296-62-095)

☐

☐

☐

4

Work/rest schedules are adjusted for temperature/wind chill (WAC 296-62-095)

☐

☐

☐

5

Buddy system is implemented for cold weather work (WAC 296-62-095)

☐

☐

☐

6

Heaters are properly ventilated to prevent CO exposure (WAC 296-155-400)

☐

☐

☐

7

Combustible materials are kept away from heaters (WAC 296-155-270)

☐

☐

☐

8

Fire extinguishers are available near heating units (WAC 296-155-260)

☐

☐

☐

9

Walkways and work areas are cleared of snow and ice (WAC 296-155-110)

☐

☐

☐

10

Sand or salt is applied to slippery surfaces (WAC 296-155-110)

☐

☐

☐

11

Equipment is winterized and operating properly (WAC 296-155-600)

☐

☐

☐

12

Vehicles have emergency winter survival kits (WAC 296-155-610)

☐

☐

☐

13

Scaffolds are cleared of snow and ice before use (WAC 296-874-200)

☐

☐

☐

14

Ladders are free of ice and snow (WAC 296-876-400)

☐

☐

☐

15

Materials are protected from freezing if required (WAC 296-155-110)

☐

☐

☐

16

Adequate lighting is provided for shorter daylight hours (WAC 296-155-165)

☐

☐

☐

17

First aid kits are stocked and accessible (WAC 296-155-120)

☐

☐

☐

18

Emergency communication methods are established (WAC 296-155-120)

☐

☐

☐

19

Workers are monitored for signs of frostbite/hypothermia (WAC 296-62-095)

☐

☐

☐

20

Warm liquids are available for workers (WAC 296-62-095)
